mechanism_fit_note: Closest industry analog: private-label canned vegetables / sauces, family-controlled dual-class, very thin float, LIFO accounting. Confirms the SENEA mechanism rather than diversifies it.
Dual-class family-controlled grocer with LIFO, owned-real-estate book, low coverage, multi-decade buyback history. Same mechanism class as SENEA across food retail.

Family-controlled dual-class department store with LIFO, owned-real-estate base, structural buybacks of historic magnitude. Coverage exists but mechanism is extreme; included to size the M2/M5/M6 confluence.
Family-controlled grocer with LIFO, owned-real-estate book, undercovered. Buyback program smaller than DDS / IMKTA.
Family-controlled (Class A super-voting) snack / nut processor; LIFO inventories; modest sell-side coverage; durable private-label-heavy customer base.
Canadian family-controlled dual-class fruit-juice / private-label processor; asset-backed; under-covered ex-Canada. Mechanism analog without the U.S. LIFO tax effect.
Family-controlled snack / frozen-dough manufacturer; LIFO; very thinly traded; small-cap-index-vulnerable.

Founding-family dual-class (super-voting) confectionery; durable niche; conservative balance sheet; persistent buybacks. Tends to trade at a premium not a discount; included as a mechanism reference, not a value comparable.
Founding-family dual-class egg producer; asset-backed agricultural niche; mechanism partial (no LIFO inventory cushion of canning scale; dividend-led capital return rather than buyback-led).
Family-controlled stainless-steel processing-equipment manufacturer; LIFO; very thin float; SEC-reporting but dark-stock-like. Strong M1/M2/M3/M4/M5 overlap with SENEA.
Steel-coil processor; LIFO; small-cap-index-vulnerable; asset-backed. Insider ownership material but not strict family-control.
Steel service center with LIFO; insider ownership material; durable mid-stream niche; periodic buyback.
